threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: Mentioning how you miss the 'soul' of your old city while sipping a latte at an independent cafe—it’s the quickest way to spot a newcomer.
Last week on the N37, a guy played the sax while we all pretended not to listen. Perfectly in tune with the rumbles of the bus beneath.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I realized my local Späti also sells fresh flowers and homemade schnapps—only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find an open-air cinema under the stars in a former bus depot in Kreuzberg every summer. It's the quirkiest vibe with a local community feel.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they casually navigate around the Civic Center dog delays like it's just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy singing opera on the 49 last week—full performance. Only in SF will a packed bus turn into a spontaneous concert.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just for bars, but the real charm is its small bakeries and fruit shops tucked away on side streets. They miss the pulse.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by the way they still hesitate at traffic lights, waiting for the green man, while locals dart across without a second thought.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's normal to complain about the bus being late. Locals just shake their heads and carry on, knowing it's part of the charm.
Caught the N37 last night, and the driver was locking eyes with a guy serenading the whole bus. It was oddly beautiful, but I bet the late-night crowd didn’t appreciate it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw my neighbor's pet goat munching on garden weeds while their toddler ran around in a superhero cape. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you stumble upon a kiosk doubling as an art gallery at 2 AM, selling currywurst and local art side by side.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their reaction to the BART delays—judging when to sprint for the bus instead of wasting time at the Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the bus stop at Turk and Jones, guitar in hand, while the Muni was late as usual. Only in SF do delays become concerts.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all square terraces are for sipping sangria, but locals actually use them for late-night guitar sessions and neighborhood gossip.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how they still squint at the metro map, holding it upside down, while locals glide past effortlessly.
